summary: Raised item translation-unit matching coverage, with five item source files flipped from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py: itrshell, itkabigon, itmatadogas, itpatapata, and ittools. The decomp.dev report recorded +5460 matched code bytes, +17576 linked code bytes, +744 matched data bytes, 23 new matches, and six fuzzy improvements in the still-unmatched itsamusgrapple TU. The PR also corrected item attribute layouts, added concrete ItemStateTable data initializers, localized several symbols in GALE01 symbols.txt, and implemented multiple previously stubbed item motion/physics/collision routines.
what_changed: configure.py changed itrshell.c, itkabigon.c, itmatadogas.c, itpatapata.c, and ittools.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).

config/GALE01/symbols.txt converted many item data labels from global it_-address symbols to local @NNN labels across .rodata, .data, .sdata, and .sdata2, including local string/float/double constants and a rodata size adjustment at 0x803B8708 from 0x10 global to 0xC local.

itCharItems.h corrected itSamusGrappleAttributes field x0C from f32 to s32, added xA4/xA8/xAC animation pointer fields, and replaced itToolsAttrEntry with itToolsMotionAttrs under itToolsAttributes::motions[1]; itToolsAttributes::xC became s32.
itCommonItems.h refined itPatapataAttributes by changing x28 to f32 and replacing pad_30 with f32 x30 and x34, and moved/added an itKabigonAttributes struct with f32/f32/f32/f32/s32/s32/f32 layout.

itkabigon.c removed placeholder/platform includes, moved the Kabigon attribute struct to the shared header, added ItemStateTable it_803F7AD8, and implemented itKabigon_UnkMotion1_Anim plus an inline helper for scale, hitlag callbacks, blast-zone handling, and camera trigger behavior.

itmatadogas.c removed placeholder/platform/debug includes, added math support and ItemStateTable initializers, implemented itMatadogas_UnkMotion1_Anim, added a PAD_STACK adjustment in UnkMotion2_Phys, implemented it_802CB4F0 item spawning with random circular velocity using cosf/sinf, and added a one-entry state table it_803F7BB0.

itpatapata.c added ItemStateTable it_803F88C0, implemented it_802E0734 and it_802E11E0, corrected the it_802E11E0 prototype to void it_802E11E0(Item_GObj*), adjusted Patapata collision code with explicit temporaries and a gobj alias marked permuterslop, and replaced an extern zero vector with a local Vec3 vel = { 0 }.

itrshell.c added x40 to the shell attrs layout, added ItemStateTable it_803F5C48, implemented velocity clamp and stop-init helpers, matched it_8028DAE4, itRshell_UnkMotion5_Phys, itRshell_UnkMotion5_Coll, and it_8028E170, using multiple static inline helpers and #pragma inline_depth(8).

ittools.c added ItemStateTable it_803F9260, changed attribute access from treating specialAttributes as an entry array to a typed itToolsAttributes wrapper with motions[], implemented itTools_UnkMotion4_Anim and it_802EF548, and updated damage/clank/shield/absorb paths to use the new typed attrs.

itsamusgrapple.h replaced an opaque hitbox-data placeholder with itSamusGrapple_HitboxData containing spawn_hitbox_0 through spawn_hitbox_4 data and bitfields; it_802B7160 now takes this typed struct.

itsamusgrapple.c added major fuzzy decompilation work for it_802B7160, it_802B75FC, it_802B7C18, it_802B9328, and it_802B99A0, plus related includes and animation hookup changes, but the TU remained in the decomp.dev report as an unmatched item with fuzzy improvements rather than being flipped to Matching.
smart_moves: Converted newly matched TUs in configure.py only after backing them with both code matches and data/state-table matches, rather than leaving them as fuzzy-only improvements.
Matched item .data by adding concrete ItemStateTable arrays next to each item implementation, preserving the standard {motion id, Anim, Phys, Coll} table shape.
Used typed attribute wrappers for Tools and Patapata to expose real field types and offsets; this let later code index attrs->motions[...] instead of pointer-arithmetic over an ambiguous entry array.
Moved itKabigonAttributes and itSamusGrapple_HitboxData into headers where callers and item code can share the same layout instead of duplicating local placeholder structs.

For Rshell, split difficult behavior into static inline helpers and used #pragma inline_depth(8), making velocity clamp, stop-init, acceleration, braking, reverse, and ground-spin logic easier to permute while preserving codegen.
Localized constants in symbols.txt to @NNN labels where the matched code/data no longer needed global it_ labels, reducing symbol-scope mismatches.
Removed unverified semantic comments from itTools attribute fields after review feedback; the final diff keeps the safer offset-style unknown field names.
decomp_lessons: A TU flip to Matching may require data-section work as much as function work: this PR matched .data/.sdata2 by adding ItemStateTable initializers and correcting local/global symbol scope.

Attribute structs should be corrected by observed layout and access types, not by guessed names; this PR accepted type/layout changes such as s32 vs f32 and named nested motion arrays, but review pushed back on invented explanatory comments.

For item matching, state tables and motion callbacks often move together: adding missing tables for itrshell, itpatapata, itmatadogas, itkabigon, and ittools made the functions and data match as complete TUs.
When decompiling physics/collision code, explicit signed temporaries, casts, ABS usage, and seemingly redundant aliases can be necessary to match compiler output.

Large character-item code such as Samus grapple can still be valuable as fuzzy progress without being marked Matching; the bot showed five Samus grapple functions reaching roughly 90-98% while configure.py left the TU nonmatching.
Do not treat AI-inferred field comments as authoritative. If semantics are not verified, retain offset/xN naming or clearly mark uncertainty rather than adding confident gameplay descriptions.
naming_conventions: Item motion callbacks continue to use it<Name>_UnkMotionN_Anim, it<Name>_UnkMotionN_Phys, and it<Name>_UnkMotionN_Coll naming, for example itRshell_UnkMotion5_Phys and itTools_UnkMotion4_Anim.

Unidentified functions remain address-based, such as it_8028DAE4, it_8028E170, it_802E0734, it_802E11E0, it_802EF548, it_802CB4F0, and Samus grapple functions it_802B7160/it_802B75FC/it_802B9328/it_802B99A0.
State table globals retain address-based names such as it_803F5C48, it_803F7AD8, it_803F7B58, it_803F7BB0, it_803F88C0, and it_803F9260.
Struct fields remain offset-style x0/x4/xC/etc. when semantics are not confirmed; type corrections are still made where needed for matching.
The PR introduced itToolsMotionAttrs and itToolsAttributes::motions[1] to express a repeated per-motion attribute entry without overclaiming individual field semantics.
Local data symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt use @NNN labels when the object should be local rather than globally named it_XXXXXXXX.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used static inline helper functions extensively to control inlining and register/stack shape in matched functions, especially in itrshell.c and itsamusgrapple.c.
Inserted PAD_STACK(...) in several functions to match stack frame size/alignment, including Matadogas, Patapata, Rshell, Tools, and Samus grapple code.
Used #pragma inline_depth(8) before it_8028DAE4 in itrshell.c to influence helper inlining for matching.
Added explicit temporaries and casts, such as s32 sign variables before multiplying floats, s32 frame counters in Patapata, and u32 casts for Tools damage caps.
Used a deliberate local alias in Patapata collision code, Item_GObj* gobj2 = gobj, annotated as permuterslop, to affect generated code without changing behavior.
Replaced an extern zero-vector reference with a local Vec3 vel = { 0 } in itpatapata.c, matching the symbol/local-constant changes in symbols.txt.
Preserved item hit state during Rshell motion changes by selecting ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE versus ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE | ITEM_HIT_PRESERVE depending on msid.
review_feedback: jellejurre questioned the semantic comments added to itToolsMotionAttrs and itToolsAttributes: "Are these comments made up by a human or by AI?"
lukechampine replied: "good catch, definitely AI guesswork that should not be taken as authoritative".
The final diff for itCharItems.h retains the layout/type/rename changes but not the questioned explanatory field comments, making this a clear precedent against unverified AI-generated comments.
The PR was approved by jellejurre before merge and later also approved by Ari4ka; no other substantive review feedback is present in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c after the fuzzy improvements to it_802B7160, it_802B75FC, it_802B9328, and it_802B99A0.
Search for other item TUs where ItemStateTable data additions enabled a configure.py flip from NonMatching to Matching.
Audit header comments introduced by AI-assisted PRs for unverified semantic claims similar to the itToolsMotionAttrs review thread.
Compare Patapata, Rshell, and Tools matching tactics for reusable PAD_STACK, inline helper, and local symbol-scope patterns.
Search for permuterslop annotations in item collision code to identify known compiler-shape workarounds.
